)

WITH RECURSIVE path_cte AS (
  -- Base case: Start with cards that have no predecessor (no other card points to them)
  SELECT id, nextcardid, id::TEXT AS path
  FROM collection c1
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM collection c2 
    WHERE c2.nextcardid = c1.id
  )
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: Follow the chain forward
  SELECT c.id, c.nextcardid, cte.path || ' --> ' || c.id::TEXT
  FROM collection c
  JOIN path_cte cte ON cte.nextcardid = c.id
)
SELECT path
FROM path_cte
WHERE nextcardid IS NULL
ORDER BY id

WITH monthly_purchases AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        user_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD')) AS purchase_month
    FROM purchase
),
streak_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        purchase_month,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY purchase_month) AS row_num,
        purchase_month - INTERVAL '1 month' * ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY purchase_month) AS streak_group
    FROM monthly_purchases
),
streak_lengths AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length
    FROM streak_groups
    GROUP BY user_id, streak_group
),
max_streaks AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        MAX(streak_length) AS longest_streak
    FROM streak_lengths
    GROUP BY user_id
)
SELECT 
    longest_streak AS streak_length,
    COUNT(*) AS num_users
FROM max_streaks
GROUP BY longest_streak
ORDER BY streak_length DESC

SELECT 
    cl.relname AS base_table,
    af.attname AS base_col,
    cr.relname AS referencing_table,
    ar.attname AS referencing_col,
    pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) AS constraint_sql
FROM pg_constraint c
JOIN pg_class cl ON c.confrelid = cl.oid  -- referenced table (users)
JOIN pg_class cr ON c.conrelid = cr.oid   -- referencing table (posts, etc.)
JOIN pg_attribute af ON c.confrelid = af.attrelid AND af.attnum = ANY(c.confkey)  -- referenced column
JOIN pg_attribute ar ON c.conrelid = ar.attrelid AND ar.attnum = ANY(c.conkey)    -- referencing column
WHERE c.confrelid = (SELECT oid FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'users')
  AND c.contype = 'f'  -- foreign key constraints only
ORDER BY referencing_table, referencing_col

    FROM superhero_missions
),
grouped_missions AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        mission_start,
        mission_end,
        SUM(CASE WHEN prev_end_time IS NULL OR prev_end_time <> mission_start THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
            OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) AS group_id

)

FROM grouped_missions
GROUP BY hero_id, group_id

(
  SELECT
    account.account_id,
    account.frequency,
    l.loan_id,
    l.date AS loan_date,
    0 AS priority
  FROM account
  LEFT JOIN loan l
    ON account.account_id = l.account_id
  WHERE account.district_id = '18'
    AND l.date >= (NOW() - INTERVAL '48 hours')
  ORDER BY l.date DESC NULLS LAST
  LIMIT 6
)
UNION
(
  SELECT
    account.account_id,
    account.frequency,
    l.loan_id,
    l.date AS loan_date,
    1 AS priority
  FROM account
  LEFT JOIN loan l
    ON account.account_id = l.account_id
  WHERE account.district_id = '18'
    AND account.account_id NOT IN (
      SELECT DISTINCT account_id
      FROM (
        SELECT
          account.account_id
        FROM account
        LEFT JOIN loan l
          ON account.account_id = l.account_id
        WHERE account.district_id = '18'
          AND l.date >= (NOW() - INTERVAL '48 hours')

        LIMIT 6

)

WITH RECURSIVE module_hierarchy AS (
  -- Get all parent modules (where id_parent_module is NULL)
  SELECT 
    id_module,
    id_parent_module,
    module_code,
    name_module,
    desc_module,
    ARRAY[]::integer[] as path
  FROM modules 
  WHERE id_parent_module IS NULL
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursively get child modules
  SELECT 
    m.id_module,
    m.id_parent_module,
    m.module_code,
    m.name_module,
    m.desc_module,
    mh.path || m.id_parent_module
  FROM modules m
  INNER JOIN module_hierarchy mh ON m.id_parent_module = mh.id_module
),
parent_modules AS (
  SELECT

  json_build_object(
    'id_module', pm.id_module,
    'id_parent_module', pm.id_parent_module,
    'module_code', pm.module_code,
    'name_module', pm.name_module,
    'desc_module', pm.desc_module,
    'children', (
      SELECT COALESCE(array_to_json(array_agg(
        json_build_object(
          'id_module', m.id_module,
          'id_parent_module', m.id_parent_module,
          'module_code', m.module_code,
          'name_module', m.name_module,
          'desc_module', m.desc_module

      )), '[]'::json)
      FROM modules m 
      WHERE m.id_parent_module = pm.id_module
    )
  )
)) AS result
FROM parent_modules pm

WITH tab_with_cardid AS (
    SELECT 
        cardid,
        CASE 
            WHEN cardid LIKE '%-%-%' THEN
                -- Extract parts before first hyphen and after second hyphen
                CONCAT(
                    SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 1), 
                    '-', 
                    SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 3),
                    CASE 
                        WHEN ARRAY_LENGTH(STRING_TO_ARRAY(cardid, '-'), 1) > 3 
                        THEN '-' || ARRAY_TO_STRING((STRING_TO_ARRAY(cardid, '-'))[4:], '-')
                        ELSE ''
                    END
                )
            ELSE cardid
        END AS pure_cardid,
        type,
        last_refresh_date
    FROM card_info
), ranked_records AS (
    SELECT 
        cardid,
        pure_cardid,
        type,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY pure_cardid ORDER BY type) AS rn
    FROM tab_with_cardid
)
SELECT 
    cardid, 
    pure_cardid,
    type AS min_type
FROM ranked_records
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY pure_cardid

        start_events.session_id,

        AND start_events.post_id = end_events.post_id

        AND end_events.event_name = 'view_end'
        AND start_events.ts < end_events.ts
)

    session_id,
    post_id,
    start_time,
    end_time,

FROM view_pairs

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION f_gap_fill_update(tbl text, id_col text, order_col text) 
RETURNS void LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $func$
DECLARE 
    tmp text[];
    col text;
    sql_stmt text;
BEGIN
    -- Get all nullable columns for the specified table
    SELECT array_agg(column_name)
    INTO tmp
    FROM information_schema.columns 
    WHERE table_name = tbl 
      AND is_nullable = 'YES'
      AND column_name NOT IN (id_col, order_col);
    
    -- Loop through each nullable column
    FOREACH col IN ARRAY tmp
    LOOP
        -- Build the UPDATE statement with forward fill logic
        sql_stmt := 'UPDATE ' || tbl || ' t1 SET ' || col || ' = t2.filled_' || col || 
                   ' FROM (SELECT ' || id_col || ', ' || order_col || ', ' || col || 
                   ', COALESCE(' || col || ', LAST_VALUE(' || col || ') IGNORE NULLS OVER ' ||
                   '(PARTITION BY ' || id_col || ' ORDER BY ' || order_col || 
                   ' ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING)) AS filled_' || col || 
                   ' FROM ' || tbl || ') t2 WHERE t1.' || id_col || ' = t2.' || id_col || 
                   ' AND t1.' || order_col || ' = t2.' || order_col || 
                   ' AND t1.' || col || ' IS NULL';
        
        -- Execute the dynamic SQL
        EXECUTE sql_stmt;
    END LOOP;
END 
$func$

WITH LastTransactionPerStation AS (
  -- Get the last transaction for each customer-gasstation combination
  SELECT *
  FROM (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC) as rn
    FROM transactions_1k
  ) t
  WHERE rn = 1
),
FirstIssueStation AS (
  -- Find the first gas station where each customer had amount < 10
  SELECT customerid,
         FIRST_VALUE(gasstationid) OVER (PARTITION BY customerid ORDER BY date ASC, time ASC) as first_issue_gasstation
  FROM transactions_1k
  WHERE amount < 10
),
CustomerHasIssues AS (
  -- Identify which customers have any transactions with amount < 10
  SELECT DISTINCT customerid
  FROM transactions_1k
  WHERE amount < 10
),
LastValidStation AS (
  -- For customers without issues, find their last valid transaction station
  SELECT ltps.customerid,
         FIRST_VALUE(ltps.gasstationid) OVER (PARTITION BY ltps.customerid ORDER BY ltps.date DESC, ltps.time DESC) as last_valid_gasstation
  FROM LastTransactionPerStation ltps
  LEFT JOIN CustomerHasIssues chi ON ltps.customerid = chi.customerid
  WHERE chi.customerid IS NULL AND ltps.amount >= 10
)
SELECT DISTINCT
  ltps.customerid,
  ltps.transactionid,
  ltps.gasstationid,
  ltps.amount
FROM LastTransactionPerStation ltps
LEFT JOIN FirstIssueStation fis ON ltps.customerid = fis.customerid
LEFT JOIN LastValidStation lvs ON ltps.customerid = lvs.customerid
WHERE 
  -- If customer has issues, show the last transaction from the first station with issues
  (fis.first_issue_gasstation IS NOT NULL AND ltps.gasstationid = fis.first_issue_gasstation)
  OR
  -- If customer has no issues, show the last transaction from their last valid station
  (fis.first_issue_gasstation IS NULL AND ltps.gasstationid = lvs.last_valid_gasstation)

WITH user_action_sequences AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        action,
        action_time,
        LEAD(action_time) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY action_time) as next_action_time
    FROM user_actions
    WHERE action IN ('LOGIN', 'SEARCH', 'BUY')
),
search_sessions AS (
    SELECT 
        s.user_id,
        s.action_time as search_time,
        l.next_action_time as session_end_time
    FROM user_action_sequences s
    JOIN user_action_sequences l ON s.user_id = l.user_id 
        AND l.action = 'LOGIN' 
        AND l.action_time < s.action_time
    WHERE s.action = 'SEARCH'
        AND (l.next_action_time IS NULL OR l.next_action_time > s.action_time)
        AND NOT EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 
            FROM user_action_sequences l2 
            WHERE l2.user_id = l.user_id 
                AND l2.action = 'LOGIN'
                AND l2.action_time > l.action_time 
                AND l2.action_time < s.action_time
        )
)
SELECT 
    ss.user_id,
    COUNT(*) as abandoned_search_count
FROM search_sessions ss
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM user_actions b 
    WHERE b.user_id = ss.user_id 
        AND b.action = 'BUY'
        AND b.action_time > ss.search_time 
        AND (ss.session_end_time IS NULL OR b.action_time < ss.session_end_time)
)
GROUP BY ss.user_id

WITH event_dates AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        DATE(e.event_date AT TIME ZONE 'UTC' AT TIME ZONE 'MST') AS event_day
    FROM event e
    JOIN attendance a ON e.event_id = a.link_to_event
    WHERE e.event_name = 'April Meeting'
),
ordered_dates AS (
    SELECT 
        event_day,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY event_day DESC) AS rn,
        DATE(event_day - INTERVAL '1 day' * ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY event_day DESC)) AS date_group
    FROM event_dates
),
streak_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        date_group,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length,
        MIN(event_day) AS start_date,
        MAX(event_day) AS end_date
    FROM ordered_dates
    GROUP BY date_group
    HAVING MAX(event_day) = (SELECT MAX(event_day) FROM ordered_dates) -- Most recent streak
)
SELECT 
    streak_length AS streak_count,
    'April Meeting' AS event_name,
    'MST' AS timezone,
    start_date,
    end_date
FROM streak_groups
ORDER BY end_date DESC

WITH RECURSIVE subgroup_connections AS (
    -- Get all items with their subgroups
    SELECT id as item, subgroup1 as subgroup_val
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
    UNION
    SELECT id as item, subgroup2 as subgroup_val
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
),
-- Create pairs of items that share subgroups
item_pairs AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT sc1.item as item1, sc2.item as item2
    FROM subgroup_connections sc1
    JOIN subgroup_connections sc2 ON sc1.subgroup_val = sc2.subgroup_val
    WHERE sc1.item <= sc2.item
),
-- Find connected components using recursive CTE
connected_components(item, component) AS (
    -- Base case: assign initial component IDs
    SELECT item, item as component
    FROM (SELECT DISTINCT item FROM subgroup_connections) items
    
    UNION
    
    -- Recursive case: propagate component IDs
    SELECT cc.item, 
           CASE WHEN ip.item2 < cc.component THEN ip.item2 ELSE cc.component END as component
    FROM connected_components cc
    JOIN item_pairs ip ON cc.item = ip.item1
),
-- Get final component assignments
final_components AS (
    SELECT item, MIN(component) as group_id
    FROM connected_components
    GROUP BY item
)
SELECT sg.id as item,
       sg.subgroup1,
       sg.subgroup2,
       fc.group_id as group
FROM superhero_group sg
JOIN final_components fc ON sg.id = fc.item
ORDER BY fc.group_id, sg.id

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to() 
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Update eff_to for all records of the same team
    UPDATE team_attributes 
    SET eff_to = subquery.next_date 
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            id,
            COALESCE(
                LEAD(date::date, 1) OVER (
                    PARTITION BY team_api_id 
                    ORDER BY date::date
                ) - 1,
                '5999-12-31'::date
            ) AS next_date
        FROM team_attributes 
        WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
    ) AS subquery
    WHERE team_attributes.id = subquery.id;
    
    RETURN NULL;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER after_insert_team_attributes 
AFTER INSERT ON team_attributes 
FOR EACH ROW 
EXECUTE PROCEDURE update_team_attributes_eff_to()

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_school_inventory_after_supply() 
RETURNS TRIGGER 
LANGUAGE plpgsql 
AS $trigger$ 
BEGIN 
    -- Update existing inventory record or insert new one if it doesn't exist
    INSERT INTO school_inventory (cdscode, supply_name, quantity)
    VALUES (NEW.cdscode, NEW.supply_name, NEW.quantity)
    ON CONFLICT (cdscode, supply_name) 
    DO UPDATE SET 
        quantity = school_inventory.quantity + NEW.quantity,
        last_updated = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP;
    
    RETURN NEW; 
END; 
$trigger$;

WITH RECURSIVE hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: all employees with their direct managers
    SELECT 
        e.id as employee_id,
        e.salary as employee_salary,
        e.bossid as manager_id,
        1 as level
    FROM staff e
    WHERE e.bossid IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: continue up the hierarchy
    SELECT 
        h.employee_id,
        h.employee_salary,
        m.bossid as manager_id,
        h.level + 1 as level
    FROM hierarchy h
    JOIN staff m ON h.manager_id = m.id
    WHERE m.bossid IS NOT NULL
),
qualified_managers AS (
    -- Find all managers who earn at least twice as much as their subordinates
    SELECT 
        h.employee_id,
        h.employee_salary,
        h.level as hierarchical_level,
        h.manager_id as bossid,
        m.salary as boss_salary
    FROM hierarchy h
    JOIN staff m ON h.manager_id = m.id
    WHERE m.salary >= 2 * h.employee_salary
)
-- Get the closest (lowest level) qualified manager for each employee
SELECT 
    employee_id as employeeId,
    employee_salary as employeeSalary,
    hierarchical_level,
    bossid as bossId,
    boss_salary as bossSalary
FROM qualified_managers qm
WHERE qm.hierarchical_level = (
    SELECT MIN(qm2.hierarchical_level)
    FROM qualified_managers qm2
    WHERE qm2.employee_id = qm.employee_id
)
ORDER BY employee_id

WITH RECURSIVE bucketed_transfers AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT 
        id,
        amount,
        amount as cum_sum,
        1 as bucket
    FROM transfer 
    WHERE id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM transfer)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent rows
    SELECT 
        t.id,
        t.amount,
        CASE 
            WHEN bt.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN t.amount
            ELSE bt.cum_sum + t.amount 
        END as cum_sum,
        CASE 
            WHEN bt.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN bt.bucket + 1
            ELSE bt.bucket 
        END as bucket
    FROM transfer t
    JOIN bucketed_transfers bt ON t.id = (
        SELECT MIN(id) FROM transfer WHERE id > bt.id
    )
)
SELECT id, amount, cum_sum, bucket
FROM bucketed_transfers
ORDER BY id

        ROUND(count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count), 0) AS percent,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY count DESC, fundingtype) AS rn -- Tie-breaker: order by count descending, then by name
    FROM cte_funding_count

with hero_attribute(hero_id, attribute_id, attribute_value) as ( 
    values (1, 1, 80), (2, 1, 75), (3, 1, 95), (4, 1, 85), (5, 1, 90), (6, 1, 70) 
),
groups as (
    select 
        hero_id,
        attribute_value,
        case 
            when hero_id in (1,2) then 1  -- Group [1,2,3,4]
            when hero_id in (3,4) then 2  -- Group [1,2,3,4,5,6]  
            when hero_id in (5,6) then 3  -- Group [3,4,5,6]
        end as group_id
    from hero_attribute
),
expanded_groups as (
    select distinct
        g1.group_id,
        g2.hero_id,
        g2.attribute_value
    from groups g1
    cross join hero_attribute g2
    where (g1.group_id = 1 and g2.hero_id between 1 and 4) or  -- Group [1,2,3,4]
          (g1.group_id = 2 and g2.hero_id between 1 and 6) or  -- Group [1,2,3,4,5,6]
          (g1.group_id = 3 and g2.hero_id between 3 and 6)     -- Group [3,4,5,6]
)
select 
    sum(attribute_value) as sum
from expanded_groups
group by group_id
order by group_id

RETURNS TABLE (out_mine integer, out_msg text) AS $func$

    c.msg 
FROM words_chat c 
JOIN words_games g USING (gid) 
JOIN words_social s ON s.uid IN (g.player1, g.player2) 
JOIN words_users u ON u.uid = c.uid
WHERE c.gid = in_gid

WITH date_series AS (
    SELECT generate_series::date AS day_date
    FROM generate_series('2022-01-01'::timestamp, '2022-01-31'::timestamp, '1 day'::interval)
),
incident_days AS (
    SELECT 
        ds.day_date,
        ri.incident_start,
        ri.incident_end,
        -- Calculate the start of the day
        ds.day_date::timestamp AS day_start,
        -- Calculate the end of the day
        (ds.day_date + interval '1 day' - interval '1 second')::timestamp AS day_end,
        -- Find the actual start time for this day (either incident start or day start)
        GREATEST(ri.incident_start, ds.day_date::timestamp) AS effective_start,
        -- Find the actual end time for this day (either incident end or day end)
        LEAST(ri.incident_end, (ds.day_date + interval '1 day' - interval '1 second')::timestamp) AS effective_end
    FROM date_series ds
    CROSS JOIN race_incidents ri
    WHERE ri.incident_start <= (ds.day_date + interval '1 day' - interval '1 second')::timestamp
      AND ri.incident_end >= ds.day_date::timestamp
)
SELECT 
    day_date AS day_start,
    COALESCE(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (effective_end - effective_start)) / 60), 0) AS incident_minutes
FROM incident_days
GROUP BY day_date
ORDER BY day_date

    FROM budget 
    WHERE category IN ('Food', 'Advertisement')
),
cumulative_budget AS (
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        item_rank,
        SUM(amount) OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY item_rank) AS cum_amount
    FROM ranked_items
),
budget_allocation AS (
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        cum_amount,
        CASE 
            WHEN category = 'Food' AND cum_amount <= 500 THEN 'Selected'
            WHEN category = 'Advertisement' THEN 
                CASE 
                    WHEN cum_amount <= (500 - COALESCE((SELECT MAX(cum_amount) FROM cumulative_budget WHERE category = 'Food' AND cum_amount <= 500), 0)) 
                    THEN 'Selected' 
                    ELSE 'Not Selected' 
                END
            ELSE 'Not Selected'
        END AS selection_status
    FROM cumulative_budget
)
SELECT 
    budget_id,
    category,
    amount,
    cum_amount,
    selection_status
FROM budget_allocation
WHERE selection_status = 'Selected'
ORDER BY category DESC, amount ASC

WITH RECURSIVE result_calc AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT 
        id,
        trx,
        event,
        CAST(trx * event AS numeric(15, 6)) AS result_good,
        1 AS rn
    FROM (
        SELECT id, trx, event, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id) AS rn
        FROM sales_transactions
    ) t
    WHERE rn = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent rows
    SELECT 
        st.id,
        st.trx,
        st.event,
        ROUND(CAST((rc.result_good + st.trx) * st.event AS numeric(15, 6)), 6) AS result_good,
        rc.rn + 1
    FROM (
        SELECT id, trx, event, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id) AS rn
        FROM sales_transactions
    ) st
    JOIN result_calc rc ON st.rn = rc.rn + 1
    WHERE st.rn <= (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sales_transactions)
)
SELECT 
    id,
    result_good AS result
FROM result_calc
ORDER BY id

WITH daily_prices AS (
    -- Get all prices within first 15 minutes of each day for each card
    SELECT 
        card_id,
        date_value,
        price,
        DATE(date_value) as trade_date,
        EXTRACT(HOUR FROM date_value) * 60 + EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM date_value) as minute_of_day
    FROM card_prices
    WHERE EXTRACT(HOUR FROM date_value) * 60 + EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM date_value) < 15
),
first_last_prices AS (
    -- For each card and day, get first and last price within the 15-minute window
    SELECT 
        card_id,
        trade_date,
        FIRST_VALUE(price) OVER (
            PARTITION BY card_id, trade_date 
            ORDER BY date_value
        ) as first_price,
        LAST_VALUE(price) OVER (
            PARTITION BY card_id, trade_date 
            ORDER BY date_value 
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
        ) as last_price,
        COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY card_id, trade_date) as price_count
    FROM daily_prices
),
price_differences AS (
    -- Calculate difference for each card-day combination
    SELECT 
        card_id,
        trade_date,
        last_price - first_price as price_difference
    FROM first_last_prices
    WHERE price_count > 1  -- Only include days with multiple price points
)
-- Calculate average difference per card
SELECT 
    card_id,
    AVG(price_difference) as avg_difference
FROM price_differences
GROUP BY card_id
ORDER BY card_id

WITH product_attributes AS (
    SELECT 
        dp.id as datapoint_id,
        MAX(CASE WHEN var.name = 'ProductType' THEN vv.name END) AS product_type,
        MAX(CASE WHEN var.name = 'publishing_language' THEN vv.name END) AS language,
        MAX(CASE WHEN var.name = 'genre' THEN vv.name END) AS genre
    FROM datapoints dp
    JOIN datapoint_variablevalue dpvv ON dp.id = dpvv.datapoint_id
    JOIN variablevalue vv ON dpvv.variablevalue_id = vv.id
    JOIN variable var ON vv.variable_id = var.id
    GROUP BY dp.id
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(SUM(dp.value), 0) AS total_sales,
    td.years AS year,
    sd.name AS country,
    COALESCE(pa.product_type, 'Unknown') AS product_type,
    COALESCE(pa.language, 'Unknown') AS language,
    COALESCE(pa.genre, 'Unknown') AS genre
FROM time_dimension td
CROSS JOIN spatial_dimension sd
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT DISTINCT product_type, language, genre 
    FROM product_attributes
) pa
LEFT JOIN datapoints dp ON dp.time_dimension_id = td.id 
    AND dp.spatial_dimension_id = sd.id
LEFT JOIN product_attributes pa2 ON pa2.datapoint_id = dp.id
    AND pa.product_type = COALESCE(pa2.product_type, 'Unknown')
    AND pa.language = COALESCE(pa2.language, 'Unknown')
    AND pa.genre = COALESCE(pa2.genre, 'Unknown')
GROUP BY td.years, sd.name, pa.product_type, pa.language, pa.genre
ORDER BY td.years DESC, sd.name, pa.product_type, pa.language, pa.genre

WITH RECURSIVE weekdays AS (
    SELECT generate_series(0, 65) AS offset
), 
dates AS (
    SELECT 
        CURRENT_DATE + w.offset * INTERVAL '1 day' AS date,
        CASE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM CURRENT_DATE + w.offset * INTERVAL '1 day')
            WHEN 1 THEN 'Monday'
            WHEN 2 THEN 'Tuesday'
            WHEN 3 THEN 'Wednesday'
            WHEN 4 THEN 'Thursday'
            WHEN 5 THEN 'Friday'
            WHEN 6 THEN 'Saturday'
            WHEN 7 THEN 'Sunday'
        END AS day_of_week_name
    FROM weekdays w
    WHERE CURRENT_DATE + w.offset * INTERVAL '1 day' <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '2 months'
), 
schedule_blocks_with_dates AS (
    SELECT 
        sb.*, 
        d.date AS block_date
    FROM schedule_blocks sb
    JOIN dates d ON sb.day_of_week = d.day_of_week_name
    WHERE sb.is_available = TRUE 
    AND d.date >= CURRENT_DATE 
    AND d.date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '2 months'
)
SELECT 
    block_id, 
    user_id, 
    block_date AS date, 
    start_time, 
    end_time
FROM schedule_blocks_with_dates
ORDER BY date, start_time

WITH date_range AS (
  SELECT generate_series(
    DATE_TRUNC('day', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36'),
    DATE_TRUNC('day', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24'),
    interval '1 day'
  ) AS the_day
),
weekdays_only AS (
  SELECT the_day
  FROM date_range
  WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM the_day) < 6
),
time_calculation AS (
  SELECT 
    COUNT(*) * interval '1 day' AS weekday_interval
  FROM weekdays_only
  WHERE the_day BETWEEN DATE_TRUNC('day', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36') 
                    AND DATE_TRUNC('day', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24')
),
adjusted_time AS (
  SELECT 
    CASE 
      WHEN EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36') >= 6 THEN
        -- If start date is weekend, use next Monday as start
        (TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24' - 
         (TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36' + interval '3 days')) * 
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM weekdays_only) / 
        ((TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24' - TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36') / interval '1 day')
      ELSE
        TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24' - TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36'
    END AS adjusted_diff
),
final_calculation AS (
  SELECT 
    INTERVAL '1 day' * COUNT(*) AS total_weekday_time
  FROM weekdays_only
  WHERE the_day > DATE_TRUNC('day', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36')
    AND the_day < DATE_TRUNC('day', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24')
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 
    CASE 
      WHEN EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36') < 6 THEN
        (DATE_TRUNC('day', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36') + interval '1 day' - TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36')
      ELSE interval '0 seconds'
    END
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 
    CASE 
      WHEN EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24') < 6 THEN
        (TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24' - DATE_TRUNC('day', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24'))
      ELSE interval '0 seconds'
    END
)
SELECT 
  EXTRACT(DAY FROM SUM(total_weekday_time)) || ' days ' ||
  EXTRACT(HOUR FROM SUM(total_weekday_time)) || ' hours ' ||
  EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM SUM(total_weekday_time)) || ' minutes ' ||
  EXTRACT(SECOND FROM SUM(total_weekday_time)) || ' seconds ' AS time_diff
FROM final_calculation

WITH buy_transactions AS (
  SELECT 
    transaction_id,
    molecule_id,
    date,
    molecules,
    usd_total,
    usd_total / molecules as unit_price,
    SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) as cumulative_buy
  FROM transaction 
  WHERE sell = FALSE
),
sell_transactions AS (
  SELECT 
    transaction_id,
    molecule_id,
    date,
    molecules,
    usd_total,
    usd_total / molecules as unit_price,
    SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) as cumulative_sell
  FROM transaction 
  WHERE sell = TRUE
),
allocation_matrix AS (
  SELECT 
    b.molecule_id,
    b.date as buy_date,
    s.date as sell_date,
    b.cumulative_buy - b.molecules as prev_cumulative_buy,
    b.cumulative_buy,
    s.cumulative_sell - s.molecules as prev_cumulative_sell,
    s.cumulative_sell,
    b.molecules as buy_molecules,
    s.molecules as sell_molecules,
    b.unit_price,
    s.unit_price as sell_unit_price
  FROM buy_transactions b
  CROSS JOIN sell_transactions s
  WHERE b.molecule_id = s.molecule_id
    AND b.cumulative_buy > s.cumulative_sell - s.molecules
    AND b.cumulative_buy - b.molecules < s.cumulative_sell
),
final_allocations AS (
  SELECT 
    molecule_id,
    buy_date,
    sell_date,
    GREATEST(0, 
      LEAST(cumulative_buy, cumulative_sell) - 
      GREATEST(prev_cumulative_buy, prev_cumulative_sell)
    ) as allocated_molecules,
    unit_price,
    sell_unit_price
  FROM allocation_matrix
  WHERE GREATEST(0, 
      LEAST(cumulative_buy, cumulative_sell) - 
      GREATEST(prev_cumulative_buy, prev_cumulative_sell)
    ) > 0
)
SELECT 
  molecule_id,
  buy_date,
  sell_date,
  allocated_molecules as molecules,
  ROUND(allocated_molecules * unit_price, 2) as buy_usd_total,
  ROUND(allocated_molecules * sell_unit_price, 2) as sell_usd_total
FROM final_allocations
ORDER BY molecule_id, buy_date, sell_date

SELECT 
    begin_date, 
    end_date,
    CASE 
        WHEN begin_date > end_date THEN 0
        ELSE
            -- Calculate the starting point for complete months
            -- If begin_date is not the 1st, we can't count its month as complete
            -- So we start counting from the next month
            GREATEST(
                0,
                -- Calculate ending point for complete months
                -- If end_date is not the last day of its month, we can't count its month as complete
                -- So we end counting at the previous month
                (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM 
                    CASE 
                        WHEN EXTRACT(DAY FROM end_date) = EXTRACT(DAY FROM DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date) + INTERVAL '1 month - 1 day') 
                        THEN end_date 
                        ELSE DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date) - INTERVAL '1 day' 
                    END
                ) * 12 + 
                EXTRACT(MONTH FROM 
                    CASE 
                        WHEN EXTRACT(DAY FROM end_date) = EXTRACT(DAY FROM DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date) + INTERVAL '1 month - 1 day') 
                        THEN end_date 
                        ELSE DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date) - INTERVAL '1 day' 
                    END
                ))
                -
                (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM 
                    CASE 
                        WHEN EXTRACT(DAY FROM begin_date) = 1 
                        THEN begin_date 
                        ELSE DATE_TRUNC('month', begin_date) + INTERVAL '1 month' 
                    END
                ) * 12 + 
                EXTRACT(MONTH FROM 
                    CASE 
                        WHEN EXTRACT(DAY FROM begin_date) = 1 
                        THEN begin_date 
                        ELSE DATE_TRUNC('month', begin_date) + INTERVAL '1 month' 
                    END
                ))
            )
    END AS full_calendar_months

WITH RECURSIVE hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: root nodes (those with no parent)
    SELECT
        uuid,
        card_name,
        parent_uuid,
        0 as level,
        ARRAY[uuid]::uuid[] as path
    FROM card_type
    WHERE parent_uuid IS NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: child nodes
    SELECT
        ct.uuid,
        ct.card_name,
        ct.parent_uuid,
        h.level + 1,
        h.path || ct.uuid
    FROM card_type ct
    JOIN hierarchy h ON ct.parent_uuid = h.uuid
),
-- Get all nodes with their levels
all_nodes AS (
    SELECT 
        uuid,
        card_name,
        parent_uuid,
        level
    FROM hierarchy
),
-- Group children under each parent
parent_with_children AS (
    SELECT 
        p.uuid as parent_uuid,
        p.card_name as parent_name,
        COALESCE(
            json_agg(
                json_build_object(
                    'uuid', c.uuid,
                    'card_name', c.card_name
                )
                ORDER BY c.card_name
            ) FILTER (WHERE c.uuid IS NOT NULL),
            '[]'
        ) as children
    FROM (
        SELECT DISTINCT uuid, card_name
        FROM all_nodes
    ) p
    LEFT JOIN all_nodes c ON p.uuid = c.parent_uuid
    GROUP BY p.uuid, p.card_name
)
SELECT 
    parent_uuid as uuid,
    parent_name as card_name,
    children
FROM parent_with_children
ORDER BY parent_name

WITH RECURSIVE user_groups AS (
    -- First, create pairs of users that should be grouped together
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        u1.id as user1_id, 
        u2.id as user2_id
    FROM users u1
    JOIN users u2 ON u1.ref1 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref1 = u2.ref2 OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref2
    WHERE u1.id <= u2.id
    
    UNION
    
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        u1.id as user1_id, 
        u3.id as user2_id
    FROM users u1, users u3
    WHERE u1.id <= u3.id
    AND EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM users u2 
        WHERE (u1.ref1 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref1 = u2.ref2 OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref2)
        AND (u2.ref1 = u3.ref1 OR u2.ref1 = u3.ref2 OR u2.ref2 = u3.ref1 OR u2.ref2 = u3.ref2)
    )
),
grouped_users AS (
    -- Assign group IDs to connected users
    SELECT 
        u.id,
        u.username,
        u.ref1,
        u.ref2,
        COALESCE(MIN(ug.user1_id), u.id) as group_id
    FROM users u
    LEFT JOIN user_groups ug ON u.id = ug.user2_id
    GROUP BY u.id, u.username, u.ref1, u.ref2
),
user_assets AS (
    -- Get all assets with their owners, including those not in users table
    SELECT 
        a.id as asset_id,
        a.owner,
        gu.id as user_id,
        gu.username,
        gu.group_id
    FROM assets a
    LEFT JOIN users u ON a.owner = u.ref1 OR a.owner = u.ref2
    LEFT JOIN grouped_users gu ON u.id = gu.id
)
SELECT 
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ua.user_id) FILTER (WHERE ua.user_id IS NOT NULL) AS ids,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ua.username) FILTER (WHERE ua.username IS NOT NULL) AS usernames,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref1) FILTER (WHERE u.ref1 IS NOT NULL) AS refs1,

WITH admin_weeks AS (
  SELECT 
    date,
    -- Calculate the "administrative week" number
    -- Week 1 starts on the 1st of the month and ends on the first Sunday
    -- Subsequent weeks are Monday-Sunday periods
    CEIL(
      (EXTRACT(DOY FROM date) - EXTRACT(DOY FROM DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)) + 
       EXTRACT(DOW FROM DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE))) / 7.0
    ) AS admin_week_number
  FROM laboratory 
  WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date) = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) 
    AND EXTRACT(MONTH FROM date) = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE)
)
SELECT 
  admin_week_number,
  COUNT(*) as record_count
FROM admin_weeks
GROUP BY admin_week_number
ORDER BY admin_week_number

WITH RECURSIVE curricula_to_upgrade AS (
  -- Base case: start with the target curriculum
  SELECT curriculum_id
  FROM curricula
  WHERE curriculum_id = 1  -- Your target curriculum
    AND status = 'installed'

  UNION
  
  -- Recursive case: find all curricula that share resources with already identified curricula
  SELECT DISTINCT cr2.curriculum_id
  FROM curriculum_resources cr1
  JOIN curriculum_resources cr2 
    ON cr1.resource_key = cr2.resource_key 
    AND cr1.curriculum_id != cr2.curriculum_id
  JOIN curricula c 
    ON cr2.curriculum_id = c.curriculum_id
  JOIN curricula_to_upgrade ctu 
    ON cr1.curriculum_id = ctu.curriculum_id
  WHERE c.status = 'installed'  -- Only include installed curricula
)
UPDATE curricula
SET status = 'to upgrade'
WHERE curriculum_id IN (
    SELECT curriculum_id
    FROM curricula_to_upgrade
)

SELECT

        WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 END) > 0 AND COUNT(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 END) > 0 THEN 'Open'
        WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 END) > 0 THEN 'Open'
        WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 END) > 0 THEN 'Planning'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END AS consolidated_status,
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN 1 END) > 0 THEN 
            MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN remaining END)
        WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 END) > 0 AND COUNT(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 END) > 0 THEN 
            MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN spent END) + MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN spent END)
        WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 END) > 0 THEN 
            MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN spent END)
        WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 END) > 0 THEN 
            MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN spent END)

WITH co_occurrences AS (
    SELECT 
        l.cluster AS left_cluster,
        l.cluster_type AS left_type,
        l.cluster_label AS left_label,
        r.cluster AS right_cluster,
        r.cluster_type AS right_type,
        r.cluster_label AS right_label,
        l.article
    FROM tag l
    JOIN tag r ON l.article = r.article 
    WHERE l.cluster < r.cluster
),
co_occurrence_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        left_cluster,
        left_type,
        left_label,
        right_cluster,
        right_type,
        right_label,
        COUNT(DISTINCT article) AS article_count
    FROM co_occurrences
    GROUP BY left_cluster, left_type, left_label, right_cluster, right_type, right_label
),
existing_links AS (
    SELECT 
        source_cluster,
        target_cluster,
        type
    FROM link
)
SELECT 
    coc.left_cluster AS left_id,
    coc.left_type AS left_type,
    coc.left_label AS left_label,
    coc.right_cluster AS right_id,
    coc.right_type AS right_type,
    coc.right_label AS right_label,
    coc.article_count AS articles,
    ARRAY_AGG(el.type) FILTER (WHERE el.type IS NOT NULL) AS link_types
FROM co_occurrence_counts coc
LEFT JOIN existing_links el ON (
    (coc.left_cluster = el.source_cluster AND coc.right_cluster = el.target_cluster) OR
    (coc.left_cluster = el.target_cluster AND coc.right_cluster = el.source_cluster)
)
GROUP BY 
    coc.left_cluster, coc.left_type, coc.left_label,
    coc.right_cluster, coc.right_type, coc.right_label,
    coc.article_count
ORDER BY coc.article_count DESC

WITH consolidated_ranges AS (
    SELECT 
        alb,
        art,
        feat_arts,
        MIN(start_date) AS start_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN MAX(end_date) IS NULL THEN NULL
            ELSE MAX(end_date)
        END AS end_date
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            alb,
            art,
            feat_arts,
            start_date,
            end_date,
            SUM(CASE WHEN lag_end_date IS NULL OR start_date > lag_end_date + INTERVAL '1 day' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
                OVER (PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts ORDER BY start_date) AS grp
        FROM (
            SELECT 
                alb,
                art,
                feat_arts,
                start_date,
                end_date,
                LAG(end_date) OVER (PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts ORDER BY start_date) AS lag_end_date
            FROM sp_album
        ) t1
    ) t2
    GROUP BY alb, art, feat_arts, grp
)
SELECT 
    alb,
    art,
    feat_arts,
    start_date,
    end_date
FROM consolidated_ranges
ORDER BY alb, art, start_date

    -- For sets with no reprints, find the max card ID in each set
    SELECT c1.setcode, MAX(c1.id) as max_id
    FROM cards c1
    WHERE c1.setcode NOT IN (SELECT setcode FROM sets_with_reprints)
    GROUP BY c1.setcode
)

even_ranked AS (

BabyBoomerFlag AS (
    SELECT

)

FROM

island_groups AS (

WITH time_points AS (
  -- Get all unique start and end times
  SELECT DISTINCT ts FROM (
    SELECT start as ts FROM duration
    UNION 
    SELECT end as ts FROM duration
  ) t
),
intervals AS (
  -- Create intervals between consecutive time points
  SELECT 
    t1.ts as interval_start,
    MIN(t2.ts) as interval_end
  FROM time_points t1
  JOIN time_points t2 ON t1.ts < t2.ts
  GROUP BY t1.ts
)
SELECT 
  MAX(concurrent_count) as max_concurrent_events
FROM (
  SELECT 
    COUNT(d.id) as concurrent_count
  FROM intervals i
  LEFT JOIN duration d ON (
    d.start < i.interval_end 
    AND d.end > i.interval_start
  )
  GROUP BY i.interval_start, i.interval_end
) counts

WITH TeamRecords AS (
    SELECT
        team_api_id,
        id,
        date,
        buildupplayspeed,
        buildupplaypassing,
        defenceteamwidth,
        chancecreationshooting,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id ORDER BY date) AS record_order,
        COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id) AS total_records_per_team
    FROM team_attributes
    WHERE buildupplayspeed IS NOT NULL 
        AND buildupplaypassing IS NOT NULL 
        AND defenceteamwidth IS NOT NULL 
        AND chancecreationshooting IS NOT NULL
),
TeamComparisons AS (
    SELECT
        current.team_api_id,
        current.id,
        current.buildupplayspeed,
        current.buildupplaypassing,
        current.defenceteamwidth,
        current.chancecreationshooting,
        LEAD(current.buildupplayspeed) OVER (PARTITION BY current.team_api_id ORDER BY current.date) AS next_buildupplayspeed,
        LEAD(current.buildupplaypassing) OVER (PARTITION BY current.team_api_id ORDER BY current.date) AS next_buildupplaypassing,
        LAG(current.defenceteamwidth) OVER (PARTITION BY current.team_api_id ORDER BY current.date) AS prev_defenceteamwidth,
        LAG(current.chancecreationshooting) OVER (PARTITION BY current.team_api_id ORDER BY current.date) AS prev_chancecreationshooting,
        current.total_records_per_team
    FROM TeamRecords current
)
SELECT
    id,
    buildupplayspeed,
    buildupplaypassing,
    next_buildupplayspeed,
    next_buildupplaypassing,
    defenceteamwidth,
    chancecreationshooting,
    prev_defenceteamwidth,
    prev_chancecreationshooting
FROM TeamComparisons
WHERE total_records_per_team > 1
    AND prev_defenceteamwidth > buildupplayspeed
ORDER BY buildupplayspeed DESC NULLS LAST, defenceteamwidth ASC NULLS FIRST

    -- Get diagnoses from examination table
    SELECT 
        id,
        "Examination Date" AS diagnosis_date,
        diagnosis AS current_diagnosis
    FROM examination
    WHERE "Examination Date" IS NOT NULL AND diagnosis IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Get diagnoses from laboratory table

        diagnosis AS current_diagnosis
    FROM laboratory
    WHERE date IS NOT NULL AND diagnosis IS NOT NULL
),
ordered_diagnoses AS (
    SELECT

    FROM all_diagnoses

filtered_records AS (
    SELECT

    -- For consecutive rows, (rn - row_number over dates) will be constant

        rn,

WITH RECURSIVE set_hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: Start with sets that have parents
    SELECT 
        id,
        code,
        parentcode,
        code::text AS ancestral_path,
        1 AS level
    FROM sets 
    WHERE parentcode IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: Join with parent sets to build the path
    SELECT 
        sh.id,
        sh.code,
        s.parentcode,
        sh.ancestral_path || '-' || s.code AS ancestral_path,
        sh.level + 1
    FROM set_hierarchy sh
    JOIN sets s ON sh.parentcode = s.code
    WHERE s.parentcode IS NOT NULL
),
-- Find the complete paths (where we've reached the top of the hierarchy)
complete_paths AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        code,
        ancestral_path
    FROM set_hierarchy sh1
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 
        FROM set_hierarchy sh2 
        WHERE sh2.id = sh1.id 
        AND sh2.level > sh1.level
    )
),
-- Handle cases where we need to add the ultimate parent
final_paths AS (
    SELECT 
        cp.id,
        cp.code,
        CASE 
            WHEN s.parentcode IS NOT NULL THEN cp.ancestral_path || '-' || s.code
            ELSE cp.ancestral_path
        END AS ancestral_path
    FROM complete_paths cp
    LEFT JOIN sets s ON cp.ancestral_path LIKE '%' || s.code 
        AND s.code = (
            SELECT s2.code 
            FROM sets s2 
            WHERE s2.code = SPLIT_PART(cp.ancestral_path, '-', (
                SELECT COUNT(*) 
                FROM unnest(string_to_array(cp.ancestral_path, '-')) AS x
            ))
        )
        AND s.parentcode IS NULL
)
SELECT 
    id,
    code,
    CASE 
        WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sets s WHERE s.code = SPLIT_PART(final_paths.ancestral_path, '-', 1) AND s.parentcode IS NULL)
        THEN final_paths.ancestral_path
        ELSE final_paths.ancestral_path || '-' || (
            SELECT s.parentcode 
            FROM sets s 
            WHERE s.code = SPLIT_PART(final_paths.ancestral_path, '-', 1)
        )
    END AS ancestral_path
FROM final_paths

UNION ALL

-- Simpler approach - let me rewrite this more clearly
WITH RECURSIVE set_lineage AS (
    -- Anchor: Start with all sets that have parents
    SELECT 
        id,
        code,
        parentcode,
        code::text AS ancestral_path,
        0 AS depth
    FROM sets
    WHERE parentcode IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive: Continue until we reach a set with no parent
    SELECT 
        sl.id,
        sl.code,
        s.parentcode,
        s.code || '-' || sl.ancestral_path AS ancestral_path,
        sl.depth + 1
    FROM set_lineage sl
    JOIN sets s ON sl.parentcode = s.code
    WHERE sl.depth < 10 -- Prevent infinite recursion
)
SELECT DISTINCT
    id,
    code,
    ancestral_path
FROM set_lineage
WHERE depth = (
    SELECT MAX(depth) 
    FROM set_lineage sl2 
    WHERE sl2.id = set_lineage.id
)
ORDER BY id

WITH match_dates AS (
    SELECT m.country_id,
           m.date,
           m.league_id,
           m.season,
           DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY m.country_id ORDER BY m.date DESC) AS match_rank
    FROM "match" m
    WHERE m.date IS NOT NULL
),
country_stats AS (
    SELECT c.id AS country_id,
           c.name AS country_name,
           COUNT(DISTINCT m.league_id) AS league_count,
           COUNT(DISTINCT m.season) AS season_count
    FROM "country" c
    LEFT JOIN "match" m ON c.id = m.country_id
    GROUP BY c.id, c.name
),
match_summary AS (
    SELECT md.country_id,
           MAX(CASE WHEN md.match_rank = 1 THEN md.date END) AS last_match_date,
           MAX(CASE WHEN md.match_rank = 2 THEN md.date END) AS prev_match_date,
           STRING_AGG(CASE WHEN md.match_rank = 1 THEN l.name END, ', ' ORDER BY l.name) AS last_match_leagues,
           STRING_AGG(CASE WHEN md.match_rank = 2 THEN l.name END, ', ' ORDER BY l.name) AS prev_match_leagues
    FROM match_dates md
    JOIN "league" l ON md.league_id = l.id
    WHERE md.match_rank <= 2
    GROUP BY md.country_id
)
SELECT cs.country_id,
       cs.country_name,
       ms.last_match_date,
       ms.last_match_leagues,
       ms.prev_match_date,
       ms.prev_match_leagues,
       cs.league_count,
       cs.season_count
FROM country_stats cs
JOIN match_summary ms ON cs.country_id = ms.country_id
ORDER BY ms.last_match_date DESC

LEFT JOIN bond AS b ON c.bond_id = b.bond_id 
WHERE a1.molecule_id = a2.molecule_id 
  AND a1.atom_id != a2.atom_id

WITH OrderedRecords AS (
    SELECT
        cdscode,
        school,
        fundingtype,
        lastupdate,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY cdscode ORDER BY lastupdate) AS rn
    FROM schools
    WHERE cdscode IS NOT NULL
),

Comparisons AS (
    SELECT
        curr.cdscode,
        curr.lastupdate,
        prev.school AS old_school,
        curr.school AS new_school,
        prev.fundingtype AS old_fundingtype,
        curr.fundingtype AS new_fundingtype
    FROM OrderedRecords curr
    LEFT JOIN OrderedRecords prev 
        ON curr.cdscode = prev.cdscode 
        AND curr.rn = prev.rn + 1
    WHERE prev.cdscode IS NOT NULL  -- Exclude first records (no previous record to compare)
)

SELECT
    cdscode,
    'School' AS ColumnName,
    COALESCE(old_school, '') AS OldValue,
    COALESCE(new_school, '') AS NewValue
FROM Comparisons
WHERE old_school IS DISTINCT FROM new_school

UNION ALL

SELECT
    cdscode,
    'FundingType' AS ColumnName,
    COALESCE(old_fundingtype, '') AS OldValue,
    COALESCE(new_fundingtype, '') AS NewValue
FROM Comparisons
WHERE old_fundingtype IS DISTINCT FROM new_fundingtype

ORDER BY
    cdscode,
    CASE WHEN ColumnName = 'School' THEN 1 ELSE 2 END

WITH RECURSIVE T_period_futur AS
  (SELECT 1 AS Tpf_period_id,

   UNION ALL SELECT Tpf_period_id + 1 AS Tpf_period_id,
                    Tpf_start_period + INTERVAL '1 week'
   FROM T_period_futur
   WHERE Tpf_period_id < 12 )
SELECT Tpf_period_id,
       Tpf_start_period,
       EXTRACT(YEAR FROM Tpf_start_period) AS Tpf_year,
       CAST(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM Tpf_start_period) AS VARCHAR) || '.' || TO_CHAR(Tpf_start_period, 'IW') AS Tpf_period_number,
       Tpf_start_period + INTERVAL '6 days' AS Tpf_end_period
FROM T_period_futur

WITH monthly_last_exams AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        "Examination Date",
        thrombosis,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', "Examination Date")::DATE AS exam_month,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY id, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "Examination Date"), EXTRACT(MONTH FROM "Examination Date")
            ORDER BY "Examination Date" DESC
        ) AS rn
    FROM examination
),
filtered_exams AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        "Examination Date",
        thrombosis,
        exam_month
    FROM monthly_last_exams
    WHERE rn = 1
),
with_previous AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        "Examination Date",
        exam_month,
        thrombosis,
        LAG(thrombosis) OVER (
            PARTITION BY id 
            ORDER BY "Examination Date"
        ) AS prev_thrombosis
    FROM filtered_exams
)
SELECT 
    id,
    exam_month AS startofmonth,
    CASE 
        WHEN prev_thrombosis IS NOT NULL AND prev_thrombosis < thrombosis THEN 'Yes'
        ELSE 'No'
    END AS increase_risk,
    CASE 
        WHEN prev_thrombosis IS NOT NULL AND prev_thrombosis > thrombosis THEN 'Yes'
        ELSE 'No'
    END AS decrease_risk
FROM with_previous
ORDER BY id, exam_month

    TO_CHAR(TO_DATE("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS'), 'IW')::INTEGER AS week,
    SUM(CASE
            WHEN "home_team_goal" > "away_team_goal" THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END) AS home_wins,
    SUM(CASE
            WHEN "home_team_goal" < "away_team_goal" THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END) AS away_wins,
    SUM(CASE
            WHEN "home_team_goal" = "away_team_goal" THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END) AS draws,
    COUNT(*) AS total_matches
FROM "match"
WHERE "date" IS NOT NULL 
    AND "home_team_goal" IS NOT NULL 
    AND "away_team_goal" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_DATE("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')),
         TO_CHAR(TO_DATE("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS'), 'IW')
ORDER BY year, week

WITH RaceResults AS (

        r.raceId, 
        r.year, 
        r.name AS raceName, 
        res.positionText, 
        d.forename || ' ' || d.surname AS driverName, 
        res.driverId,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY r.raceId, res.positionText ORDER BY res.resultId NULLS FIRST) AS position_rank 
    FROM results AS res 
    INNER JOIN races AS r ON res.raceId = r.raceId 
    INNER JOIN drivers AS d ON res.driverId = d.driverId 
    WHERE res.positionText IN ('1', '2', '3')
)
SELECT 
    rr.raceId, 
    MAX(rr.year) AS year, 
    MAX(rr.raceName) AS raceName, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN rr.positionText = '1' THEN rr.driverName END) AS position1_driver, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN rr.positionText = '1' THEN rr.driverId END) AS position1, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN rr.positionText = '2' THEN rr.driverName END) AS position2_driver, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN rr.positionText = '2' THEN rr.driverId END) AS position2, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN rr.positionText = '3' THEN rr.driverName END) AS position3_driver, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN rr.positionText = '3' THEN rr.driverId END) AS position3 
FROM RaceResults AS rr

INSERT INTO groups (room_guid, user_guid)
SELECT 
    l.guid AS room_guid,
    g.user_guid AS user_guid
FROM login l
JOIN (
    -- Get login/name pairs that have duplicates
    SELECT login, name
    FROM login
    GROUP BY login, name
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
) duplicates ON l.login = duplicates.login AND l.name = duplicates.name
JOIN groups g ON g.room_guid IN (
    -- Find the GUIDs from the same duplicate group that are already in groups table
    SELECT l2.guid
    FROM login l2
    WHERE l2.login = l.login AND l2.name = l.name
    AND l2.guid IN (SELECT room_guid FROM groups)
)
WHERE l.guid NOT IN (SELECT room_guid FROM groups)

WITH RECURSIVE ExpSequence AS (
    SELECT 
        exp_id,
        mid,
        qty,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY exp_id) AS rn
    FROM experiment 
    WHERE mid = 2
),
ExecutionCheck AS (
    -- Base case: first experiment
    SELECT 
        exp_id,
        mid,
        qty,
        rn,
        CASE 
            WHEN qty <= (SELECT qty FROM inventory WHERE mid = 2) 
            THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS is_executable,
        CASE 
            WHEN qty <= (SELECT qty FROM inventory WHERE mid = 2) 
            THEN (SELECT qty FROM inventory WHERE mid = 2) - qty
            ELSE (SELECT qty FROM inventory WHERE mid = 2)
        END AS remaining_qty
    FROM ExpSequence
    WHERE rn = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent experiments
    SELECT 
        e.exp_id,
        e.mid,
        e.qty,
        e.rn,
        CASE 
            WHEN e.qty <= ec.remaining_qty THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS is_executable,
        CASE 
            WHEN e.qty <= ec.remaining_qty THEN ec.remaining_qty - e.qty
            ELSE ec.remaining_qty
        END AS remaining_qty
    FROM ExpSequence e
    INNER JOIN ExecutionCheck ec ON e.rn = ec.rn + 1
)
SELECT 
    exp_id,
    mid,
    is_executable
FROM ExecutionCheck
ORDER BY exp_id

WITH PaymentRanks AS (
    SELECT 
        payments,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY payments DESC) AS dense_rank_num,
        COUNT(DISTINCT payments) OVER () AS distinct_payment_count,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS total_count
    FROM loan
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(*) = 0 THEN NULL
        WHEN MAX(distinct_payment_count) = 1 THEN NULL
        WHEN MAX(total_count) < 2 THEN NULL
        ELSE MAX(CASE WHEN dense_rank_num = 2 THEN payments END)
    END AS SecondHighest
FROM PaymentRanks

WITH
    set_a_filtered AS (
        SELECT DISTINCT a.molecule_id
        FROM atom a
        WHERE EXISTS (
            SELECT 1
            FROM bond b
            WHERE b.molecule_id = a.molecule_id 
            AND b.bond_id = 'TR011_2_24'
        )
        AND EXISTS (
            SELECT 1
            FROM connected c
            JOIN atom a1 ON c.atom_id = a1.atom_id
            JOIN atom a2 ON c.atom_id2 = a2.atom_id
            WHERE a1.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
            AND a2.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
            AND a1.element = a2.element
        )
        GROUP BY a.molecule_id
        HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.element) > 1
    ),
    
    set_a AS (
        SELECT 
            af.molecule_id,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT a.element) DESC, af.molecule_id) AS rank_num
        FROM set_a_filtered af
        JOIN atom a ON af.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
        GROUP BY af.molecule_id
    ),
    
    set_b AS (
        SELECT 
            m.molecule_id,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT a.element) DESC, m.molecule_id) AS rank_num
        FROM molecule m
        JOIN atom a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
        WHERE m.label IS NOT NULL
        GROUP BY m.molecule_id
    )

SELECT
    COUNT(CASE WHEN a.molecule_id IS NOT NULL AND b.molecule_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS common_count,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN a.molecule_id IS NOT NULL AND b.molecule_id IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS only_in_a_count,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN a.molecule_id IS NULL AND b.molecule_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS only_in_b_count
FROM set_a a
FULL OUTER JOIN set_b b ON a.rank_num = b.rank_num

          CASE
              WHEN POSITION(LOWER('Aaron') IN LOWER(P.PLAYER_NAME)) > 0 THEN 10
              ELSE 0
          END AS AARON_SCORE,
          CASE
              WHEN POSITION(LOWER('Cresswell') IN LOWER(P.PLAYER_NAME)) > 0 THEN 5
              ELSE 0
          END AS CRESSWELL_SCORE,
          CASE
              WHEN POSITION(LOWER('Doran') IN LOWER(P.PLAYER_NAME)) > 0 THEN 0.1
              ELSE 0
          END AS DORAN_SCORE
   FROM PLAYER AS P)
SELECT ID,
       PLAYER_NAME AS DESCRIPTION,
       (AARON_SCORE + CRESSWELL_SCORE + DORAN_SCORE) AS SCORE
FROM KEYWORDMATCHES

ORDER BY SCORE DESC

WITH monthly_periods AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    rate,
    LOWER(time_span)::DATE AS start_date,
    COALESCE(UPPER(time_span)::DATE, CURRENT_DATE) AS end_date
  FROM budgets
  WHERE LOWER(time_span) IS NOT NULL
),
month_ranges AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    rate,
    start_date,
    end_date,
    GENERATE_SERIES(
      DATE_TRUNC('month', start_date),
      DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date),
      '1 month'
    )::DATE AS month_start
  FROM monthly_periods
),
monthly_calculations AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    rate,
    month_start,
    GREATEST(start_date, month_start) AS effective_start,
    LEAST(end_date, (month_start + INTERVAL '1 month - 1 day')::DATE) AS effective_end,
    EXTRACT('days' FROM (LEAST(end_date, (month_start + INTERVAL '1 month - 1 day')::DATE) - GREATEST(start_date, month_start))) + 1 AS days_in_month
  FROM month_ranges
  WHERE GREATEST(start_date, month_start) <= LEAST(end_date, (month_start + INTERVAL '1 month - 1 day')::DATE)
)
SELECT 
  GENERATE_SERIES(effective_start, effective_end, '1 day')::DATE AS date,
  ROUND(rate / days_in_month, 2) AS daily_rate
FROM monthly_calculations
ORDER BY date

WITH RECURSIVE "my_table" AS (
  SELECT 'A' AS "cto", 1 AS "nif", 'A1' AS "file_", 5 AS "filesize"
  UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 1, 'A2', 3
  UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 2, 'A3', 2
  UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 2, 'A4', 1
  UNION ALL SELECT 'B', 1, 'B1', 4
  UNION ALL SELECT 'B', 1, 'B2', 6
),
ordered_data AS (
  SELECT 
    "cto",
    "nif", 
    "file_",
    "filesize",
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY "cto" ORDER BY SUBSTR("file_", 1, 1), CAST(SUBSTR("file_", 2) AS INTEGER)) AS rn,
    COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY "cto", "nif") AS nif_count,
    SUM("filesize") OVER (PARTITION BY "cto", "nif") AS nif_total_size
  FROM "my_table"
),
grouped_nifs AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT
    "cto",
    "nif",
    MIN(rn) AS first_rn,
    COUNT(*) AS nif_count,
    SUM("filesize") AS nif_total_size
  FROM ordered_data
  GROUP BY "cto", "nif"
),
partition_builder AS (
  -- Base case: start with first nif group
  SELECT 
    g."cto",
    g."nif",
    g.first_rn,
    g.nif_count,
    g.nif_total_size,
    1 AS partition_num,
    g.nif_total_size AS partition_size,
    g.first_rn AS partition_start_rn,
    g.first_rn + g.nif_count - 1 AS partition_end_rn,
    ARRAY[g."nif"] AS nifs_in_partition
  FROM grouped_nifs g
  WHERE g.first_rn = (
    SELECT MIN(first_rn) 
    FROM grouped_nifs g2 
    WHERE g2."cto" = g."cto"
  )
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: add next nif group
  SELECT 
    next_g."cto",
    next_g."nif",
    next_g.first_rn,
    next_g.nif_count,
    next_g.nif_total_size,
    CASE 
      WHEN current_p.partition_size + next_g.nif_total_size > 10 
        OR ARRAY_LENGTH(current_p.nifs_in_partition, 1) + next_g.nif_count > 7
      THEN current_p.partition_num + 1
      ELSE current_p.partition_num
    END AS partition_num,
    CASE 
      WHEN current_p.partition_size + next_g.nif_total_size > 10 
        OR ARRAY_LENGTH(current_p.nifs_in_partition, 1) + next_g.nif_count > 7
      THEN next_g.nif_total_size
      ELSE current_p.partition_size + next_g.nif_total_size
    END AS partition_size,
    CASE 
      WHEN current_p.partition_size + next_g.nif_total_size > 10 
        OR ARRAY_LENGTH(current_p.nifs_in_partition, 1) + next_g.nif_count > 7
      THEN next_g.first_rn
      ELSE current_p.partition_start_rn
    END AS partition_start_rn,
    next_g.first_rn + next_g.nif_count - 1 AS partition_end_rn,
    CASE 
      WHEN current_p.partition_size + next_g.nif_total_size > 10 
        OR ARRAY_LENGTH(current_p.nifs_in_partition, 1) + next_g.nif_count > 7
      THEN ARRAY[next_g."nif"]
      ELSE current_p.nifs_in_partition || next_g."nif"
    END AS nifs_in_partition
  FROM grouped_nifs next_g
  JOIN partition_builder current_p ON next_g."cto" = current_p."cto"
  WHERE next_g.first_rn = (
    SELECT MIN(first_rn) 
    FROM grouped_nifs g2 
    WHERE g2."cto" = current_p."cto" 
    AND g2.first_rn > current_p.first_rn
  )
),
final_partitions AS (
  SELECT 
    od."cto",
    od."nif",
    od."file_",
    od."filesize",
    od.rn,
    pb.partition_num,
    SUM(od."filesize") OVER (PARTITION BY od."cto", pb.partition_num) AS total_partition_size
  FROM ordered_data od
  JOIN partition_builder pb ON od."cto" = pb."cto" AND od."nif" = pb."nif"
),
ranked_rows AS (
  SELECT 
    "cto",
    "nif", 
    "file_",
    "filesize",
    partition_num,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY "cto", partition_num ORDER BY rn) AS subpartition_num,
    total_partition_size
  FROM final_partitions
)
SELECT 
  "cto",
  "nif",
  "file_",
  partition_num,
  subpartition_num,
  total_partition_size
FROM ranked_rows
ORDER BY "cto", partition_num, subpartition_num

WITH date_events AS (
  -- Get all start and end events
  SELECT 
    dt,
    event_type,
    delta
  FROM (
    SELECT 
      valid_from AS dt,
      'start' AS event_type,
      1 AS delta
    FROM (
      SELECT TO_DATE('2014-01-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_from,
             TO_DATE('2014-01-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_until
      FROM schools
      LIMIT 1
      UNION ALL 
      SELECT TO_DATE('2014-02-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_from,
             TO_DATE('2014-02-28', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_until
      FROM schools
      LIMIT 1
    ) ranges
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
      valid_until + INTERVAL '1 day' AS dt,
      'end' AS event_type,
      -1 AS delta
    FROM (
      SELECT TO_DATE('2014-01-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_from,
             TO_DATE('2014-01-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_until
      FROM schools
      LIMIT 1
      UNION ALL 
      SELECT TO_DATE('2014-02-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_from,
             TO_DATE('2014-02-28', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_until
      FROM schools
      LIMIT 1
    ) ranges
  ) events
),
cumulative_counts AS (
  -- Calculate cumulative count at each event point
  SELECT 
    dt,
    SUM(delta) OVER (ORDER BY dt, event_type DESC) AS running_count,
    LEAD(dt) OVER (ORDER BY dt, event_type DESC) AS next_dt
  FROM date_events
  ORDER BY dt, event_type DESC
)
SELECT 
  dt AS valid_from,
  CASE 
    WHEN next_dt IS NOT NULL THEN next_dt - INTERVAL '1 day'
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS valid_until,
  running_count AS count
FROM cumulative_counts
WHERE running_count > 0
  AND (next_dt IS NULL OR next_dt > dt)

WITH RECURSIVE molecule_traversal AS (
    -- Start with the first atom (lowest atom_id)
    SELECT 
        a.atom_id,
        a.element,
        c.atom_id2 as connected_atom_id,
        c.bond_id,
        1 as level,
        ARRAY[a.atom_id] as path
    FROM atom a
    LEFT JOIN connected c ON a.atom_id = c.atom_id
    WHERE a.molecule_id = 'TR000'
    ORDER BY a.atom_id
    LIMIT 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursively follow connections
    SELECT 
        a.atom_id,
        a.element,
        c.atom_id2 as connected_atom_id,
        c.bond_id,
        mt.level + 1,
        mt.path || a.atom_id
    FROM molecule_traversal mt
    JOIN connected c ON mt.connected_atom_id = c.atom_id 
        AND NOT c.atom_id2 = ANY(mt.path)  -- Avoid cycles
    JOIN atom a ON c.atom_id2 = a.atom_id
    WHERE a.molecule_id = 'TR000'
    AND mt.level < 10  -- Prevent infinite recursion
)
SELECT DISTINCT
    mt.atom_id,
    mt.element,
    b.bond_id,
    b.bond_type
FROM molecule_traversal mt
LEFT JOIN bond b ON mt.bond_id = b.bond_id
WHERE mt.atom_id IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY mt.level

WITH RECURSIVE vt as (
    SELECT t.*, 
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid ORDER BY startdate, transactionid) as seqnum
    FROM transactions_1k t
),
cte as (
    -- Base case: first transaction for each customer
    SELECT vt.transactionid, 
           vt.customerid, 
           vt.startdate, 
           vt.enddate, 
           vt.seqnum,
           vt.enddate as reference_end_date,  -- This will be used for comparing subsequent transactions
           0 as flag  -- First transaction is always unflagged
    FROM vt
    WHERE seqnum = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: process subsequent transactions
    SELECT vt.transactionid, 
           vt.customerid, 
           vt.startdate, 
           vt.enddate, 
           vt.seqnum,
           -- Determine new reference date: if current transaction is unflagged, use its end date, otherwise keep previous reference
           CASE 
               WHEN vt.startdate <= (cte.reference_end_date + interval '90 days') THEN cte.reference_end_date
               ELSE vt.enddate
           END as reference_end_date,
           -- Flag current transaction: 1 if within 90 days of reference, 0 otherwise
           CASE 
               WHEN vt.startdate <= (cte.reference_end_date + interval '90 days') THEN 1 
               ELSE 0 
           END as flag
    FROM cte 
    JOIN vt ON vt.seqnum = cte.seqnum + 1 AND vt.customerid = cte.customerid
)
SELECT cte.transactionid, 
       cte.customerid, 
       cte.startdate, 
       cte.enddate, 
       cte.flag
FROM cte
ORDER BY cte.customerid, cte.startdate

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (
    -- Base case: start with the given IDs, track both the current ID and the original starting ID
    SELECT id as current_id, parentid, id as original_id
    FROM laboratorylink
    WHERE id IN (657266, 115562)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: follow the parent links upward
    SELECT l.id as current_id, l.parentid, c.original_id
    FROM laboratorylink l
    JOIN cte c ON l.id = c.parentid
    WHERE l.parentid IS NOT NULL
)
-- Select only the ultimate ancestors (where parentid is NULL) and show which original ID they correspond to
SELECT original_id, current_id as ultimate_ancestor_id
FROM cte
WHERE parentid IS NULL
ORDER BY original_id

weekly_groups AS (

        enddate,
        contracttype,
        LAG(enddate) OVER (PARTITION BY driverid, contracttype ORDER BY startdate) AS prev_enddate,
        LAG(contracttype) OVER (PARTITION BY driverid ORDER BY startdate) AS prev_contracttype
    FROM drivercontracts
),
grouped_contracts AS (
    SELECT 
        driverid,
        startdate,
        enddate,
        contracttype,
        SUM(CASE 
            WHEN prev_enddate IS NULL OR 
                 prev_contracttype != contracttype OR 
                 prev_enddate < startdate - INTERVAL '1 day' 
            THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END) OVER (PARTITION BY driverid ORDER BY startdate ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) AS group_id
    FROM ordered_contracts
)
SELECT 
    driverid,
    MIN(startdate) AS startdate,
    MAX(enddate) AS enddate,
    contracttype
FROM grouped_contracts
GROUP BY driverid, contracttype, group_id
ORDER BY driverid, startdate

WITH 
-- First, merge overlapping blackout periods to create consolidated blackout ranges
merged_blackout_periods AS (
    SELECT 
        start_date,
        end_date
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            start_date,
            end_date,
            SUM(CASE WHEN start_date > max_end_so_far THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (ORDER BY start_date) AS group_id
        FROM (
            SELECT 
                start_date,
                end_date,
                MAX(end_date) OVER (ORDER BY start_date ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING) AS max_end_so_far
            FROM blackout_periods
            WHERE start_date IS NOT NULL AND end_date IS NOT NULL
        ) t
    ) grouped
    GROUP BY group_id
),

-- Create inverted blackout periods (non-blackout periods)
inverse_blackout_periods AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(LAG(end_date) OVER (ORDER BY start_date), '1900-01-01') + INTERVAL '1 day' AS start_date,
        start_date - INTERVAL '1 day' AS end_date
    FROM merged_blackout_periods
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        MAX(end_date) + INTERVAL '1 day' AS start_date,
        '9999-01-01'::date AS end_date
    FROM merged_blackout_periods
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        '1900-01-01'::date AS start_date,
        MIN(start_date) - INTERVAL '1 day' AS end_date
    FROM merged_blackout_periods
),

-- Filter valid inverse periods
valid_inverse_periods AS (
    SELECT start_date, end_date
    FROM inverse_blackout_periods
    WHERE start_date <= end_date
      AND start_date >= '1900-01-01'
      AND end_date <= '9999-01-01'
),

-- Get all active periods from account_activity
active_periods AS (
    SELECT start_date, end_date
    FROM account_activity
    WHERE start_date IS NOT NULL AND end_date IS NOT NULL
)

-- Finally, find intersection of active periods with non-blackout periods
SELECT 
    GREATEST(a.start_date, i.start_date) AS start_date,
    LEAST(a.end_date, i.end_date) AS end_date
FROM active_periods a
INNER JOIN valid_inverse_periods i
    ON a.start_date <= i.end_date 
    AND i.start_date <= a.end_date
ORDER BY start_date

WITH atom_data AS (
    SELECT 
        atom_id as id,
        molecule_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN element = 'cl' THEN UPPER(element) 
            ELSE element 
        END AS val
    FROM atom
),
bond_data AS (
    SELECT 
        bond_id as id,
        molecule_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN bond_type = '-' THEN 'single' 
            WHEN bond_type = '=' THEN 'double' 
            WHEN bond_type = '#' THEN 'triple' 
            ELSE bond_type 
        END AS val
    FROM bond
),
combined_data AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        molecule_id,
        val
    FROM atom_data
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        id,
        molecule_id,
        val
    FROM bond_data
),
molecule_elements AS (
    SELECT 
        m.molecule_id,
        string_agg(
            CASE 
                WHEN a.element = 'cl' THEN UPPER(a.element) 
                ELSE a.element 
            END, 
            ',' ORDER BY a.atom_id
        ) AS elements
    FROM molecule m
    LEFT JOIN atom a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
    GROUP BY m.molecule_id
)
SELECT 
    cd.id,
    cd.molecule_id,
    cd.val,
    me.elements AS molecule_composition
FROM combined_data cd
LEFT JOIN molecule_elements me ON cd.molecule_id = me.molecule_id
ORDER BY cd.molecule_id, cd.id

tier_calculations AS (
    SELECT 
        tr.tier_start,
        tr.tier_end,
        tr.rate,
        CASE 
            WHEN iq.quantity >= tr.tier_start THEN 
                LEAST(iq.quantity, tr.tier_end) - tr.tier_start + 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS applicable_quantity
    FROM tiered_rates tr
    CROSS JOIN input_quantity iq
    WHERE iq.quantity >= tr.tier_start
)
SELECT 
    SUM(tc.applicable_quantity * tc.rate) AS total_cost
FROM tier_calculations tc
WHERE tc.applicable_quantity > 0

   UNION ALL SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",

   UNION ALL SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",

   UNION ALL SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",
                    'SWOFFBALCR' AS "ASSET_TYPE",
                    2 AS "PRIORITY"
   UNION ALL SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",
                    'SWOFFBALDR' AS "ASSET_TYPE",
                    2 AS "PRIORITY"
   UNION ALL SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",
                    'SWFWNOTLCR' AS "ASSET_TYPE",

WITH src(id,geom) as (
    values 
        (1, 'linestring(0 0, 0 10)'::geometry), 
        (2, 'linestring(0 10, 0 11)'::geometry), 
        (3, 'linestring(1 1, 0 5)'::geometry), 
        (4, 'linestring(1 1, 2 2)'::geometry)
)
SELECT DISTINCT a.id, b.id as intersecting_id, st_asText(st_intersection(a.geom, b.geom)) as intersection_point
FROM src AS a 
JOIN src AS b ON a.id != b.id 
WHERE ST_Intersects(a.geom, b.geom)
AND NOT (
    ST_Touches(a.geom, b.geom) 
    AND (
        ST_Equals(ST_StartPoint(a.geom), ST_Intersection(a.geom, b.geom))
        OR ST_Equals(ST_EndPoint(a.geom), ST_Intersection(a.geom, b.geom))
        OR ST_Equals(ST_StartPoint(b.geom), ST_Intersection(a.geom, b.geom))
        OR ST_Equals(ST_EndPoint(b.geom), ST_Intersection(a.geom, b.geom))
    )
)

WITH "MaxHeightCTE" AS (

    FROM superhero 
    GROUP BY "alignment_id", "publisher_id"
),
"JoinedCTE" AS (
    SELECT s."id", s."superhero_name", s."alignment_id", s."publisher_id", 
           s."height_cm", s."hair_colour_id" 
    FROM superhero AS s 
    JOIN "MaxHeightCTE" AS m 
        ON s."alignment_id" = m."alignment_id" 
        AND s."publisher_id" = m."publisher_id" 
        AND s."height_cm" = m."max_height"
),
"GroupSumCTE" AS (
    SELECT "alignment_id", "publisher_id", 
           SUM("hair_colour_id") AS "total_hair_colour"
    FROM superhero
    GROUP BY "alignment_id", "publisher_id"
)
SELECT j."id", j."superhero_name", j."alignment_id", j."publisher_id", 
       j."height_cm", j."hair_colour_id", 
       g."total_hair_colour"
FROM "JoinedCTE" AS j

    ON j."alignment_id" = g."alignment_id" 
    AND j."publisher_id" = g."publisher_id"

         j."superhero_name" NULLS FIRST

        WHEN a."element" = 'h' THEN 'Hydrogen'

WITH RECURSIVE time_segments AS (
  -- Base case: start with the original event intervals
  SELECT 
    id,
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    start_timestamp,
    end_timestamp,
    duration,
    start_timestamp as current_start,
    end_timestamp as current_end
  FROM event_usage
  WHERE start_timestamp BETWEEN '2024-10-01' AND '2024-10-09'
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: split intervals that cross day boundaries
  SELECT 
    id,
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    start_timestamp,
    end_timestamp,
    duration,
    DATE_TRUNC('day', current_start) + INTERVAL '1 day' as current_start,
    current_end
  FROM time_segments
  WHERE DATE_TRUNC('day', current_start) + INTERVAL '1 day' <= current_end
),
working_hours_calculation AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    current_start,
    current_end,
    -- Calculate the working hours portion for each segment
    CASE 
      -- If it's a weekend, no working hours
      WHEN EXTRACT(DOW FROM current_start) IN (0, 6) THEN 0
      ELSE
        -- Calculate overlap with 9:00-18:00 working hours
        GREATEST(
          0,
          EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
            LEAST(current_end, DATE_TRUNC('day', current_start) + INTERVAL '18 hours') -
            GREATEST(current_start, DATE_TRUNC('day', current_start) + INTERVAL '9 hours')
          )
        )
    END AS working_seconds
  FROM time_segments
)
SELECT 
  learner_code, 
  resource_key, 
  SUM(working_seconds) AS total_time_spent
FROM working_hours_calculation
GROUP BY learner_code, resource_key
ORDER BY learner_code, resource_key

WITH latest_team_attributes AS (
    SELECT
        team_api_id,
        buildupplayspeed,
        date,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id ORDER BY date DESC) as rn
    FROM team_attributes
    WHERE buildupplayspeed IS NOT NULL
),
match_with_speed AS (
    SELECT
        m.id,
        m.date,
        m.home_team_api_id,
        m.away_team_api_id,
        m.home_team_goal,
        m.away_team_goal,
        t.team_long_name as home_team_name,
        t2.team_long_name as away_team_name,
        ta.buildupplayspeed as home_speed
    FROM match m
    JOIN team t ON m.home_team_api_id = t.team_api_id
    JOIN team t2 ON m.away_team_api_id = t2.team_api_id
    JOIN latest_team_attributes ta ON m.home_team_api_id = ta.team_api_id AND ta.rn = 1
    WHERE m.date BETWEEN '2015-01-01' AND '2016-01-01'
)
SELECT
    id,
    date,
    home_team_api_id,
    away_team_api_id,
    home_team_goal,
    away_team_goal,
    home_team_name,
    away_team_name,
    home_speed
FROM match_with_speed
WHERE home_speed > (
    SELECT AVG(buildupplayspeed) 
    FROM team_attributes 
    WHERE buildupplayspeed IS NOT NULL
)
ORDER BY home_team_goal DESC, away_team_goal ASC

WITH match_dates AS
  (SELECT "id",
          TO_TIMESTAMP("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') AS match_start_time,
          TO_CHAR(TO_TIMESTAMP("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS'), 'ID') AS day_of_week_num,
          TO_TIMESTAMP("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') + INTERVAL '2 hours' AS match_end_time
   FROM "match"
   WHERE "date" IS NOT NULL)
SELECT m.*,
       CASE 
         WHEN m.day_of_week_num IN ('1', '2', '3', '4', '5') THEN
           ROUND(
             GREATEST(
               LEAST(
                 EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM m.match_end_time),
                 EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM DATE_TRUNC('day', m.match_start_time) + INTERVAL '17 hours')
               ) - 
               GREATEST(
                 EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM m.match_start_time),
                 EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM DATE_TRUNC('day', m.match_start_time) + INTERVAL '7 hours 30 minutes')
               ),
               0
             ) / 60
           )
         ELSE 0
       END AS match_duration_minutes
FROM match_dates m
WHERE m.day_of_week_num IN ('1', '2', '3', '4', '5')

WITH school_data AS (
  SELECT
    s.cdscode AS "pk",
    CASE
      WHEN s.charter = 1 THEN 'Charter'
      WHEN s.magnet = 1 THEN 'Magnet'
      ELSE 'Regular'
    END AS "col2",
    SUBSTRING(s.county FROM 1 FOR POSITION(' ' IN s.county) - 1) AS "col3",
    TO_TIMESTAMP(TO_CHAR(s.lastupdate, 'YYYY/MM/DD') || ' ' ||
           LPAD(CAST(MOD(ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY s.cdscode), 24) AS TEXT), 2, '0') || ':' ||
           LPAD(CAST(MOD(ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY s.cdscode) * 7, 60) AS TEXT), 2, '0'),
           'YYYY/MM/DD HH24:MI') AS "col4"
  FROM schools s
  WHERE s.statustype = 'Active'
    AND s.lastupdate IS NOT NULL
),
filtered_data AS (
  SELECT
    sd."pk",
    sd."col2",
    sd."col3",
    sd."col4"
  FROM school_data sd
  WHERE sd."col4" >= TO_TIMESTAMP('2024/09/04 00:00', 'YYYY/MM/DD HH24:MI')
    AND sd."col4" < TO_TIMESTAMP('2024/09/04 00:05', 'YYYY/MM/DD HH24:MI')
),
aggregated_data AS (
  SELECT
    fd."col2",
    STRING_AGG(DISTINCT fd."col3", ', ' ORDER BY fd."col3") AS "col3_list",
    COUNT(fd."pk") AS "record_count"
  FROM filtered_data fd
  GROUP BY fd."col2"
)

SELECT
  ad."col2",
  ad."col3_list" AS "col3",
  ad."record_count"
FROM aggregated_data ad
ORDER BY ad."record_count" DESC
