SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN count >= 0 AND count < 21 THEN '0-21'
        WHEN count >= 21 AND count < 34 THEN '21-34'
        WHEN count >= 34 AND count < 64 THEN '34-64'
        WHEN count >= 64 THEN '64+'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END AS range,
    AVG(count) AS avg_count
FROM tags
WHERE count IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN count >= 0 AND count < 21 THEN '0-21'
        WHEN count >= 21 AND count < 34 THEN '21-34'
        WHEN count >= 34 AND count < 64 THEN '34-64'
        WHEN count >= 64 THEN '64+'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END
ORDER BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN '0-21' THEN 1
        WHEN '21-34' THEN 2
        WHEN '34-64' THEN 3
        WHEN '64+' THEN 4
        ELSE 5
    END

WITH player_date_ranges AS (
    SELECT
        player_fifa_api_id as id,
        MIN(date::date) AS first_date,
        MAX(date::date) AS last_date
    FROM player_attributes
    WHERE player_fifa_api_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY player_fifa_api_id
),
gap_analysis AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        CASE 
            WHEN first_date > '2000-01-01' THEN 'Before First Date'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS gap_type,
        CASE 
            WHEN first_date > '2000-01-01' THEN '2000-01-01'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS reference_date,
        first_date AS activity_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN first_date > '2000-01-01' THEN first_date - '2000-01-01'::date
            ELSE NULL
        END AS gap_days
    FROM player_date_ranges
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        id,
        CASE 
            WHEN last_date < '2025-12-31' THEN 'After Last Date'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS gap_type,
        last_date AS activity_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN last_date < '2025-12-31' THEN '2025-12-31'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS reference_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN last_date < '2025-12-31' THEN '2025-12-31'::date - last_date
            ELSE NULL
        END AS gap_days
    FROM player_date_ranges
)
SELECT 
    id,
    gap_type,
    activity_date,
    gap_days
FROM gap_analysis
WHERE gap_type IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY id, gap_type

WITH match_times AS (
    SELECT
        "match_api_id",
        "date",
        CAST("date" AS TIMESTAMP) AS match_timestamp,
        DATE(CAST("date" AS TIMESTAMP)) AS match_date,
        EXTRACT(HOUR FROM CAST("date" AS TIMESTAMP)) +
        EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM CAST("date" AS TIMESTAMP))/60 AS hour_of_day
    FROM "match"
),
target_times AS (
    SELECT 8 AS target_hour, 'morning' AS time_period
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 20 AS target_hour, 'evening' AS time_period
),
distance_calc AS (
    SELECT
        mt."match_api_id",
        mt."date",
        mt.match_date,
        mt.match_timestamp,
        tt.target_hour,
        tt.time_period,
        ABS(mt.hour_of_day - tt.target_hour) AS time_distance,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY mt.match_date, tt.time_period
            ORDER BY ABS(mt.hour_of_day - tt.target_hour), mt."match_api_id"
        ) AS rn
    FROM match_times mt
    CROSS JOIN target_times tt
)
SELECT
    dc.match_date,
    dc.time_period,
    dc."match_api_id",
    dc."date" AS match_datetime,
    dc.target_hour,
    dc.time_distance
FROM distance_calc dc
WHERE dc.rn = 1
ORDER BY
    dc.match_date,
    dc.time_period

WITH team_matches AS (
    -- Get all matches with home team perspective
    SELECT 
        m.id,
        m.date,
        m.home_team_api_id as team_api_id,
        m.home_team_goal,
        m.away_team_goal,
        m.home_team_goal - m.away_team_goal as goal_difference
    FROM match m
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Get all matches with away team perspective
    SELECT 
        m.id,
        m.date,
        m.away_team_api_id as team_api_id,
        m.away_team_goal,
        m.home_team_goal,
        m.away_team_goal - m.home_team_goal as goal_difference
    FROM match m
)
SELECT 
    tm.id,
    tm.team_api_id,
    t.team_long_name,
    tm.home_team_goal as goals_scored,
    tm.away_team_goal as goals_conceded,
    SUM(tm.goal_difference) OVER (
        PARTITION BY tm.team_api_id 
        ORDER BY tm.date, tm.id 
        ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING
    ) AS cumulative_goal_difference
FROM team_matches tm
JOIN team t ON tm.team_api_id = t.team_api_id
WHERE DATE(tm.date) = '2008-11-22'
ORDER BY tm.team_api_id, tm.id

WITH RECURSIVE "atom_hierarchy" AS (
    -- Base case: start with direct connections from 'TR007_8'
    SELECT 
        "atom_id", 
        "atom_id2", 
        "bond_id", 
        1 AS "level",
        ARRAY["atom_id"] AS "path"
    FROM connected 
    WHERE "atom_id" = 'TR007_8'
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: follow connections, but avoid cycles
    SELECT 
        c."atom_id", 
        c."atom_id2", 
        c."bond_id", 
        ah."level" + 1,
        ah."path" || c."atom_id"
    FROM connected AS c 
    JOIN atom_hierarchy AS ah ON c."atom_id" = ah."atom_id2"
    WHERE c."atom_id2" != ALL(ah."path")  -- Prevent cycles by checking if we've visited this node before
      AND ah."level" < 10  -- Optional: limit depth to prevent extremely deep traversals
)
SELECT 
    "atom_id", 
    "atom_id2", 
    "bond_id", 
    "level" 
FROM atom_hierarchy 
ORDER BY "level" NULLS FIRST, "atom_id" NULLS FIRST

SELECT 
    a1.molecule_id,
    a1.atom_id AS atom1_id,
    a1.element AS atom1_element,
    a2.atom_id AS atom2_id,
    a2.element AS atom2_element,
    c.bond_id,
    b.bond_type
FROM atom AS a1
INNER JOIN connected AS c ON a1.atom_id = c.atom_id
INNER JOIN atom AS a2 ON c.atom_id2 = a2.atom_id
LEFT JOIN bond AS b ON c.bond_id = b.bond_id
WHERE a1.molecule_id = a2.molecule_id
    AND a1.atom_id < a2.atom_id
ORDER BY a1.molecule_id, a1.atom_id, a2.atom_id

SELECT 
    date, 
    account_id, 
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN type IN ('PRIJEM', 'VYDAJ', 'SLUZBY') THEN type END) = 3 
        THEN 1

WITH RECURSIVE leaf_to_root AS (
    -- Base case: start with all nodes, but process leaves first
    SELECT 
        id, 
        parent_id, 
        color,
        LENGTH(id) as depth
    FROM superhero2
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: propagate colors from children to parents
    SELECT 
        p.id,
        p.parent_id,
        CASE 
            -- Count how many children the parent has with resolved colors
            WHEN (
                SELECT COUNT(*) 
                FROM superhero2 c 
                WHERE c.parent_id = p.id 
                AND c.color IS NOT NULL
            ) = 1 THEN (
                -- One child: take that child's color
                SELECT c.color 
                FROM superhero2 c 
                WHERE c.parent_id = p.id 
                AND c.color IS NOT NULL
                LIMIT 1
            )
            WHEN (
                SELECT COUNT(*) 
                FROM superhero2 c 
                WHERE c.parent_id = p.id 
                AND c.color IS NOT NULL
            ) = 2 THEN (
                -- Two children: check if colors match
                CASE 
                    WHEN (
                        SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c.color) 
                        FROM superhero2 c 
                        WHERE c.parent_id = p.id 
                        AND c.color IS NOT NULL
                    ) = 1 THEN (
                        -- Same color: use that color
                        SELECT c.color 
                        FROM superhero2 c 
                        WHERE c.parent_id = p.id 
                        AND c.color IS NOT NULL
                        LIMIT 1
                    )
                    ELSE 'gray' -- Different colors: gray
                END
            )
            ELSE p.color -- Keep existing color if not ready to process
        END as color,
        p.depth
    FROM leaf_to_root p
    WHERE EXISTS (
        -- Continue processing while there are nodes whose children all have colors
        SELECT 1 
        FROM superhero2 child 
        WHERE child.parent_id = p.id 
        AND child.color IS NOT NULL
    )
    AND p.color IS NULL -- Only process nodes that don't have a color yet
    AND NOT EXISTS (
        -- Make sure all children have been processed
        SELECT 1 
        FROM superhero2 child 
        WHERE child.parent_id = p.id 
        AND child.color IS NULL
    )
),
final_colors AS (
    -- Get the final state after all propagation
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (id) id, color
    FROM leaf_to_root
    ORDER BY id, depth DESC
)
UPDATE superhero2 
SET color = final_colors.color 
FROM final_colors 
WHERE superhero2.id = final_colors.id 
AND final_colors.color IS NOT NULL

create or replace function setup_vector_store(table_name text) returns void language plpgsql as $$
begin
    -- Drop existing function with matching name pattern
    execute format('drop function if exists match_%I(int, jsonb)', table_name);
    
    -- Create the strategies table with vector column
    execute format('create table %I (
        id bigint primary key generated always as identity,
        description text,
        metadata jsonb,
        embedding vector(1536)
    )', table_name);
    
    -- Create the matching function
    execute format('create function match_%I(
        match_count int default null,
        filter jsonb default ''{}''::jsonb,
        query_embedding vector(1536) default null
    ) returns table (
        id bigint,
        description text,
        metadata jsonb,
        similarity float
    ) language plpgsql as $func$
    begin
        return query 
        select s.id, s.description, s.metadata, 1 - (s.embedding <=> $3) as similarity
        from %I s
        where s.metadata @> $2
        order by s.embedding <=> $3
        limit $1;
    end;
    $func$', table_name, table_name);
end;
$$

WITH RECURSIVE user_date_ranges AS (
  -- Base case: start with creation date for each user
  SELECT 
    id,
    displayname,
    age,
    creationdate::date as current_date,
    lastaccessdate::date as end_date
  FROM users
  WHERE creationdate IS NOT NULL AND lastaccessdate IS NOT NULL
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: add one day until we reach end_date
  SELECT 
    id,
    displayname,
    age,
    current_date + INTERVAL '1 day',
    end_date
  FROM user_date_ranges
  WHERE current_date < end_date
),
filtered_dates AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    displayname,
    age,
    current_date
  FROM user_date_ranges
  WHERE current_date >= '2010-01-01' 
    AND current_date <= '2014-12-31'
    AND (
      -- Weekend check (0 = Sunday, 6 = Saturday)
      EXTRACT(DOW FROM current_date) IN (0, 6)
      OR
      -- Holiday checks
      (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM current_date) = 1 AND EXTRACT(DAY FROM current_date) = 1)   -- New Year's Day
      OR
      (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM current_date) = 7 AND EXTRACT(DAY FROM current_date) = 4)   -- Independence Day
      OR
      (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM current_date) = 11 AND EXTRACT(DAY FROM current_date) = 11) -- Veterans Day
      OR
      (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM current_date) = 12 AND EXTRACT(DAY FROM current_date) = 25) -- Christmas Day
    )
)
SELECT 
  id,
  displayname,
  age,
  COUNT(*) AS non_working_days
FROM filtered_dates
GROUP BY id, displayname, age
ORDER BY id

WITH RECURSIVE hierarchy_calc AS (
    -- Base case: Start with all nodes, leaf nodes have only their own emission
    SELECT 
        entityid,
        parentid,
        emission,
        emission as cumulative_emission,
        0 as level
    FROM tree_table t1
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM tree_table t2 WHERE t2.parentid = t1.entityid)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: For each parent, add weighted contributions from children
    SELECT 
        parent.entityid,
        parent.parentid,
        parent.emission,
        parent.emission + COALESCE((
            SELECT SUM(child.cumulative_emission * child.pctcontribution / 100.0)
            FROM tree_table child
            WHERE child.parentid = parent.entityid
            AND child.entityid = child.entityid
        ), 0),
        hierarchy_calc.level + 1
    FROM tree_table parent
    INNER JOIN hierarchy_calc ON parent.entityid = hierarchy_calc.parentid
),
-- Aggregate to get final values (handle duplicates from recursion)
final_results AS (
    SELECT 
        entityid,
        parentid,
        MAX(cumulative_emission) as cumulative_emission
    FROM hierarchy_calc
    GROUP BY entityid, parentid
)
SELECT 
    entityid,
    parentid,
    ROUND(cumulative_emission, 2) as cumulative_emission
FROM final_results
ORDER BY entityid NULLS FIRST

   FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy

     monthly_items AS
  (SELECT TO_CHAR(act_date, 'MON-yy') AS act_month,
          TO_CHAR(act_date, 'YYYYMM')::integer AS act_yearmonth,
          ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY act_date) AS item_id
   FROM tbl),
     months_grid AS
  (SELECT DISTINCT 
          m1.act_month AS activation_month,
          m1.act_yearmonth AS activation_yearmonth,
          m2.act_month AS actual_month,
          m2.act_yearmonth AS actual_yearmonth
   FROM monthly_items m1
   CROSS JOIN monthly_items m2
   WHERE m1.act_yearmonth <= m2.act_yearmonth),
     item_ranges AS
  (SELECT mi.item_id,
          mi.act_yearmonth,
          mg.activation_yearmonth,
          mg.actual_yearmonth
   FROM monthly_items mi
   JOIN months_grid mg ON mi.act_yearmonth >= mg.activation_yearmonth 
                        AND mi.act_yearmonth <= mg.actual_yearmonth)
SELECT mg.activation_month,
       mg.actual_month,
       COUNT(DISTINCT ir.item_id) AS active_count
FROM months_grid mg
LEFT JOIN item_ranges ir ON mg.activation_yearmonth = ir.activation_yearmonth 
                          AND mg.actual_yearmonth = ir.actual_yearmonth
GROUP BY mg.activation_month, mg.actual_month, mg.activation_yearmonth, mg.actual_yearmonth
ORDER BY mg.activation_yearmonth, mg.actual_yearmonth
